title: luzula campestris subsp. vulgaris
intro: Luzula campestris subsp. vulgaris, also known as the Field Woodrush, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the family Juncaceae. It is a perennial herb that grows in damp meadows and fields, and has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to reduce swelling and prevent infection in wounds. The roots and leaves of the Field Woodrush can be prepared as a decoction or tincture for use in herbal remedies, which may help to promote digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues.
